subject: Gaming
title: The Atari 2600 Revolution
description: A short lesson to help you apply The Atari 2600 Revolution.

hook.text: The Atari 2600 put arcade games in your living room. By 1982, one in four American homes had one.

buildup.text: Released in 1977 for $199, it used swappable cartridges—play different games on the same machine.

discovery.text: The home port of Space Invaders sold over a million copies—the first killer app for a console.

twist.text: Third-party developers flooded the market with terrible games. Quality control simply didn't exist.

climax.text: The Atari 2600 sold 30 million units and proved the home console market was real.

punchline.text: Arcades in your living room. The home gaming era began here.

quiz.question: What made the Atari 2600 revolutionary?
options: Interchangeable cartridges let you play different games on one machine
It could connect to the internet
